## Issue to Resolve

experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already be reported!

<!--- Provide a code example, or an unambiguous set of steps to -->
<!--- reproduce this bug. Minimize the example to the bare -->
<!--- minimum required to reproduce the bug. Small, well written -->
<!--- example are proven to be resolved quicker. -->

```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
        AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

will reformat to:
```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction(): AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

The rule may need to make an exception for parameterless functions, or honor the max line length.

- `CHANGELOG.md`

content.thinking:
**Identifying whitespace fix conflict with line breaks**

The rule enforcing space after colon wrongly merges lines when colon is followed by newline indentation, breaking line-length and formatting; to fix this, spacing corrections should skip replacing newlines with spaces to preserve line breaks and respect max line length.**Planning inspection of related rules**

Planning to check other rules for handling newlines after colons to see if multiline return types are accepted and how spacing rules interact, aiming for consistent behavior.

 36→public class SpacingAroundCurlyRule : StandardRule("curly-spacing") {
 37→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 38→        node: ASTNode,
 39→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 40→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 41→    ) {
 42→        if (node.isLeaf() && !node.isPartOfString()) {
 43→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
 44→            val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
 45→            val spacingBefore: Boolean
 46→            val spacingAfter: Boolean
 47→            if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
 48→                spacingBefore =
 49→                    prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace ||
 50→                    prevLeaf?.elementType == AT ||
 51→                    (
 52→                        prevLeaf?.elementType == LPAR &&
 53→                            ((node as LeafPsiElement).parent is KtLambdaExpression || node.parent.parent is KtLambdaExpression)
 54→                        )
 55→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || nextLeaf?.elementType == RBRACE
 56→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace &&
 57→                    !prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 58→                    prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let {
 59→                        it.elementType == LPAR || it.elementType == AT
 60→                    } == true
 61→                ) {
 62→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 63→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 64→                        prevLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf.node)
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 68→                    (
 69→                        prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let { it.elementType == RPAR || KtTokens.KEYWORDS.contains(it.elementType) } == true ||
 70→                            node.treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 71→                            (prevLeaf.treeParent.elementType == FUN && prevLeaf.treeNext.elementType != LAMBDA_EXPRESSION)
 72→                        ) // allow newline for lambda return type
 73→                ) {
 74→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected newline before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 75→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                        val eolCommentExists = prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let {
 77→                            it is PsiComment && it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT
 78→                        } ?: false
 79→                        if (eolCommentExists) {
 80→                            val commentLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf()!!
 81→                            if (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() is PsiWhiteSpace) {
 82→                                (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() as LeafPsiElement).rawRemove()
 83→                            }
 84→                            (node.treeParent.treeParent as TreeElement).removeChild(commentLeaf)
 85→                            (node.treeParent as TreeElement).addChild(commentLeaf, node.treeNext)
 86→                            node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 87→                        }
 88→                        (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
 89→                    }
 90→                }
 91→            } else if (node.elementType == RBRACE) {
 92→                spacingBefore = prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || prevLeaf?.elementType == LBRACE
 93→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf == null || nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf)
 94→                if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !nextLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 95→                    shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf.nextLeaf())
 96→                ) {
 97→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space after \"${node.text}\"", true)
 98→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 99→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
100→                    }
101→                }
102→            } else {
103→                return
104→            }
105→            when {
106→                !spacingBefore && !spacingAfter -> {
107→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing around \"${node.text}\"", true)
108→                    if (autoCorrect) {
109→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
110→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
111→                    }
112→                }
113→                !spacingBefore -> {
114→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing before \"${node.text}\"", true)
115→                    if (autoCorrect) {
116→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
117→                    }
118→                }
119→                !spacingAfter -> {
120→                    emit(node.startOffset + 1, "Missing spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
121→                    if (autoCorrect) {
122→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
123→                    }
124→                }
125→            }
126→        }
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(leaf: ASTNode?): Boolean {
130→        val nextElementType = leaf?.elementType
131→        return (
132→            nextElementType == DOT ||
133→                nextElementType == COMMA ||
134→                nextElementType == RBRACKET ||
135→                nextElementType == RPAR ||
136→                nextElementType == SEMICOLON ||
137→                nextElementType == SAFE_ACCESS ||
138→                nextElementType == EXCLEXCL ||
139→                nextElementType == LBRACKET ||
140→                nextElementType == LPAR ||
141→                nextElementType == COLONCOLON
142→            )
143→    }
144→}

content.thinking:
**Preventing newline-to-space replacement in spacing rule**

The function return type spacing rule should avoid replacing newline whitespace with a single space to prevent violating max line length, even if that means allowing newlines; this keeps minimal changes and defers max line length enforcement to other rules.**Adjusting return type spacing to preserve newlines**

The fix will ensure that if whitespace after the colon includes a newline, the rule won't emit or replace it—only normalizing whitespace when it lacks newlines—to allow multiline formatting without breaking max line length. This minimal change keeps return types on separate lines intact and adds space only when missing.

 36→public class SpacingAroundCurlyRule : StandardRule("curly-spacing") {
 37→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 38→        node: ASTNode,
 39→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 40→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 41→    ) {
 42→        if (node.isLeaf() && !node.isPartOfString()) {
 43→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
 44→            val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
 45→            val spacingBefore: Boolean
 46→            val spacingAfter: Boolean
 47→            if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
 48→                spacingBefore =
 49→                    prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace ||
 50→                    prevLeaf?.elementType == AT ||
 51→                    (
 52→                        prevLeaf?.elementType == LPAR &&
 53→                            ((node as LeafPsiElement).parent is KtLambdaExpression || node.parent.parent is KtLambdaExpression)
 54→                        )
 55→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || nextLeaf?.elementType == RBRACE
 56→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace &&
 57→                    !prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 58→                    prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let {
 59→                        it.elementType == LPAR || it.elementType == AT
 60→                    } == true
 61→                ) {
 62→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 63→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 64→                        prevLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf.node)
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 68→                    (
 69→                        prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let { it.elementType == RPAR || KtTokens.KEYWORDS.contains(it.elementType) } == true ||
 70→                            node.treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 71→                            (prevLeaf.treeParent.elementType == FUN && prevLeaf.treeNext.elementType != LAMBDA_EXPRESSION)
 72→                        ) // allow newline for lambda return type
 73→                ) {
 74→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected newline before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 75→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                        val eolCommentExists = prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let {
 77→                            it is PsiComment && it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT
 78→                        } ?: false
 79→                        if (eolCommentExists) {
 80→                            val commentLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf()!!
 81→                            if (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() is PsiWhiteSpace) {
 82→                                (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() as LeafPsiElement).rawRemove()
 83→                            }
 84→                            (node.treeParent.treeParent as TreeElement).removeChild(commentLeaf)
 85→                            (node.treeParent as TreeElement).addChild(commentLeaf, node.treeNext)
 86→                            node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 87→                        }
 88→                        (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
 89→                    }
 90→                }
 91→            } else if (node.elementType == RBRACE) {
 92→                spacingBefore = prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || prevLeaf?.elementType == LBRACE
 93→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf == null || nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf)
 94→                if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !nextLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 95→                    shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf.nextLeaf())
 96→                ) {
 97→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space after \"${node.text}\"", true)
 98→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 99→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
100→                    }
101→                }
102→            } else {
103→                return
104→            }
105→            when {
106→                !spacingBefore && !spacingAfter -> {
107→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing around \"${node.text}\"", true)
108→                    if (autoCorrect) {
109→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
110→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
111→                    }
112→                }
113→                !spacingBefore -> {
114→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing before \"${node.text}\"", true)
115→                    if (autoCorrect) {
116→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
117→                    }
118→                }
119→                !spacingAfter -> {
120→                    emit(node.startOffset + 1, "Missing spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
121→                    if (autoCorrect) {
122→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
123→                    }
124→                }
125→            }
126→        }
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(leaf: ASTNode?): Boolean {
130→        val nextElementType = leaf?.elementType
131→        return (
132→            nextElementType == DOT ||
133→                nextElementType == COMMA ||
134→                nextElementType == RBRACKET ||
135→                nextElementType == RPAR ||
136→                nextElementType == SEMICOLON ||
137→                nextElementType == SAFE_ACCESS ||
138→                nextElementType == EXCLEXCL ||
139→                nextElementType == LBRACKET ||
140→                nextElementType == LPAR ||
141→                nextElementType == COLONCOLON
142→            )
143→    }
144→}
